text.title: Rust HTTP Handlers (Axum) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for rust-http-handlers. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale. Last rule is the terminal skip-this-methodology guard.

rule.statement: Every handler MUST consume and produce a typed DTO; raw String / serde_json::Value bodies forbidden outside debug routes.
rule.rationale: Typed DTOs catch shape drift at compile time; raw JSON drifts silently.

rule.statement: Handlers MUST receive shared resources via State<Arc<AppState>>; never instantiate clients inside the handler.
rule.rationale: Per-handler client creation defeats connection pooling and balloons latency.

rule.statement: Every fallible handler returns Result<_, AppError>; AppError implements IntoResponse mapping to RFC 7807.
rule.rationale: Single error path keeps HTTP response shape consistent across the service.

rule.statement: Every handler MUST carry #[tracing::instrument] (or equivalent) with request fields skipped where appropriate.
rule.rationale: Without per-handler spans, debugging tail latency requires correlating logs by hand.

rule.statement: Public-facing AppError messages MUST be scrubbed of stack traces, SQL strings, and internal IDs; full detail stays in tracing fields.
rule.rationale: Internal detail leaking via 500 bodies is a recurring security finding.

rule.statement: If the preconditions in `Applies If` fail or any of the `Skip If` conditions is true, return `skipped` and apply no further rules.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are not universal; running them outside their fit-window wastes effort and produces wrong outputs.
